zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Ros於大約1262年出生在英國越可爾爾姆斯利時，她的父親Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley34歲，母親Isabel d'Aubigny30歲。她和John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1348年10月13日在英國素福爾可布倫德斯頓享年87歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Ros于大约1262年出生在英国越可爾爾姆斯利时，她的父亲Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley34岁，母亲Isabel d'Aubigny30岁。她和John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel至少有3个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1348年10月13日在英国素福爾可布倫德斯頓享年87岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Ros was born about 1262, in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, her father, Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley, was 34 and her mother, Isabel d'Aubigny, was 30. She had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel. She died on 13 October 1348, in Blundeston, Suffolk, England, at the age of 87.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Ros est née environ 1262, à Helmsley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Isabel d'Aubigny, avait 30 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel. Elle est décédée le 13 octobre 1348, à Blundeston, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 87 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Ros um 1262 in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley 34 und ihre Mutter Isabel d'Aubigny 30. Sie hatte mit John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 13. Oktober 1348 im Alter von 87 Jahren in Blundeston, Suffolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Ros è nata il circa 1262 nel Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Isabel d'Aubigny, aveva 30 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia con John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel. Morì il 13 ottobre 1348, nel Blundeston, Suffolk, England, all'età di 87 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Rosが約1262年にイギリスヨークシュアイアヘルムスリーで生まれた時，父Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsleyは34歳，母Isabel d'Aubignyは30歳であった。彼女はJohn Lovell 1st Baron Lovelとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1348年10月13日にイギリススフォルクブルンデストンで87歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Ros(이)가 대략 1262년 영국 요르크실르 헬슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel d'Aubigny(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1348년 10월 13일 영국 수폴크 블런데스턴에서 87세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Ros nasceu aproximadamente 1262, em Helmsley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Isabel d'Aubigny, tinha 30 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha com John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel. Ela faleceu em 13 de outubro de 1348, em Blundeston, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 87 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Ros родилась около 1262 г. в местности хелмсли, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Isabel d'Aubigny, 30. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь от John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel. Она умерла 13 октября 1348 г. в местности блундестон, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 87 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Ros nació aproximadamente 1262, en Helmsley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Isabel d'Aubigny, tenía 30 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija con John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel. Murió el 13 de octubre de 1348, en Blundeston, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 87 años.
